🚨 Exit Alerts

⚠️ NVDA — WATCH — 2.1% from stop

Stop: $204 (just below $200–207 support zone) · Current: $208.44 · Distance: 2.1%

Stop re-derived: $200–207 is the real support zone (pre-breakout base + 200-day MA region $190). Stored $204 sits inside the zone — not ideal. Real exit trigger: decisive break of $200 on volume.

TP1: $236.54 (52-week high / real ATH resistance) · TP2: $260 (analyst cluster) · TP3: $285 (BofA/bull-case)

Technical: Below 50-day MA $212.30 (bearish signal). Price is drifting on low volume post-NVDA $25B bond announcement (+3.5% June 15, now giving it back). No panic selling yet.

Action: NO TRIM — locked in at −$12.43 loss. Selling adds $3 commission on top of the loss. If $200 zone breaks on heavy volume → exit immediately. Hold and watch tonight.

⚠️ AVGO — WATCH — 3.8% from stop

Stop: $379 (below 50-day MA $386.88) · Current: $393.94 · Distance: 3.8%

TP1 (re-derived): $418 (200-day MA reclaim — real overhead resistance) · TP2: $460 (prior cluster) · TP3: $495 (June 3 ATH)

Context: Crashed −14% from $495 ATH after Q2 FY26 earnings — revenue +48% YoY, AI semi +143% YoY. Sell-off was guidance-driven (Q3 outlook missed highest buy-side expectations). 46 analysts, avg target $522. Fundamental thesis intact.

Action: NO TRIM — at −$5.70 loss. Key: if 50-day MA ($386.88) breaks on volume → stop at $379 executes. If holds, next leg targets $418 (200-day MA). Stored TP1 $474 is now above the ATH — updated to $418.

✅ LLY — HOLD (above TP1, pulling back from ATH)

Real stop: $1,034 (200-day MA · stored $915 has no structure behind it) · Current: ~$1,135 · TP1: $1,114 ✅ HIT · TP2: $1,243

ATH $1,182.73 hit June 8. Now at ~$1,135. 50-day MA $1,140 is slight overhead resistance. Orforglipron oral GLP-1 launch (Q2 2026) is the next catalyst leg.

Action: HOLD. A 30% trim (0.029 shares) nets $1.14 after $3 commission — too thin to act. Missed the $1,182 spike trim opportunity; the window is past. Hold for TP2 ($1,243) on the next leg. Watch $1,114 TP1 as support on the pullback.

✅ TSM — HOLD (at resistance)

Real stop: $412 (below support $415.17 · stored $357 has no structure) · Current: $425.83 · Resistance: $426.80 · 52-wk high: $450.16

Sitting directly at resistance $426.80. Clean break above = next target $450 (52-week high) then analyst consensus $467.84. Geopolitical tail risk (Taiwan Strait) unchanged — remains the binary override.

Action: HOLD. Let it break or pull back to $415 support. Do not exit into resistance. Monitor Taiwan Strait news daily.

⏳ Pending Watchlist — Waiting on IBKR Approval

Both positions broker-blocked. VRDN window is closing fast — 13 days.

🔴 URGENT: 13 days to PDUFA · Veligrotug for TED · June 30, 2026

🚨 13 days to PDUFA. IBKR approval needed immediately. If not approved in time, this window closes. When approved: enter $240 at market (~$16–18), stop $14.50.

Approval probability: 89%

Halal: ⚠️ Unverified — check Musaffa (pre-revenue biotech, term debt structure needs verification)

BTD + Priority Review + p<0.0001 Phase 3 + FDA eGFR alignment + $5.28M director open-market buy. Zero CRLs in IgAN indication historically. Q3 2026 eGFR readout = second catalyst leg. Entry price $34.26 essentially at target.

Priority: VERA over VRDN for IBKR approval — 20-day window vs 13, higher conviction (89% vs 82%), entry price still valid. When approved: enter $240 at ~$34, stop $24.50. Your call.

💵 Cash Deployment

Cash: $213.81 — after $6 reserve + $3 buy commission: $204.81 deployable

⚠️ Both catalyst plays (VRDN, VERA) are broker-blocked. Cash cannot reach highest-conviction ideas until IBKR approves.

Option A — VRDN if IBKR approves: Enter $204 at ~$16–18 (original $20 target has moved, enter at market). Stop $14.50. Event June 30. 13 days left.

Option B — VERA if IBKR approves: Enter $204 at ~$34. Stop $24.50. Event July 7. 20 days left. Higher conviction, more time, entry at target.

Option C — Hold dry powder: NVDA near stop — if $200 zone breaks, freed $287 deploys into VERA directly (timed almost perfectly with July 7 PDUFA).

Recommended: Hold cash. Priority action: resolve IBKR approval today. VERA is the target — entry valid, 20 days remaining. VRDN's 13-day window is tighter. Don't deploy into ASML or existing positions just to put cash to work. Your call.

🔄 Rotation Suggestions

No profitable exits available today.

· NVDA: −$12.43 loss. Selling locks in loss + $3 commission. Must hold.

· AVGO: −$5.70 loss. Same constraint.

· LLY: 30% trim nets only $1.14 after $3 commission. Not actionable.

· TSM: Profitable +$8.40, but stop is 16% away and price is at resistance — hold for TP ($450).

Hidden rotation trigger: If NVDA $200 stop hits, freed $287 gross naturally rotates into VERA ahead of July 7 PDUFA. That's the forced rotation path if NVDA breaks down. Not a suggestion — just the natural outcome of the setup.

📚 Concept #21 of 30 — US Equity Mechanics

Bid-Ask Spread: Stocks vs Your FX World

In FX you got used to 0.5–2 pip spreads on EURUSD — essentially zero friction. US equity mega-caps work similarly: NVDA's bid-ask spread is $0.01–$0.02 on a $208 stock (5–10bps). You are paying nothing meaningful. But it breaks down fast on smaller names. VRDN at $16.34 likely runs a $0.05–$0.10 spread (30–60bps), and after-hours on any name can blow out to 200–500bps even on NVDA. Rule: use market orders freely on NVDA, AVGO, LLY, TSM during 9:30–16:00 ET. Switch to limit orders on anything below $50 with thin float. And when IBKR approves VRDN or VERA, do not hit market on entry — use a limit order at the ask or 1–2 cents above. On a 13-day pre-PDUFA window, the spread on a $16 name can eat 40bps of your return before you've done anything.

Also today: NBBO (National Best Bid Offer) — the consolidated best bid/ask across all US exchanges. The benchmark for whether your fill was fair. If your broker fills you outside NBBO without explanation, that is a reportable issue.

Research only — not financial advice. Halal status verified AAOIFI as of May 2026; quarterly re-verification due August 2026.
